steps.title: Identify the time frame and tense pattern
steps.content: The passage opens with “Trade was vital” and “which reigned in Southeast Asia from around 600 CE to 1200 CE” — marking a historical period entirely in the past. The next sentence must describe what the people did during that same period. The final sentence confirms this pattern: “they received valuable items” is also past tense. The passage establishes a consistent past-tense narrative throughout.

steps.title: Match the verb tense to the historical narrative
steps.content: Produced is past tense, matching the tense of was, reigned, and received. The other three options (are producing, produce, will produce) shift the narrative to present or future tense, breaking the tense consistency required when narrating historical events from a single time period. That matches choice C — C is correct.
